source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Help Build the Future of Healthcare AI

Infinitus is AI communications in service of patients.

Healthcare is inherently complex and siloed. We’re pioneering agentic communications to connect the entire system — any stakeholder, any channel, inbound and out. The result is intelligent care that reduces patient anxiety, increases speed to treatment, and enables teams to scale impact without stretching resources.

Our system combines industry-leading safety guardrails with the largest dynamic knowledge graph built on millions of real-world interactions. It’s why 44% of the healthcare Fortune 50 trusts Infinitus to turn fragmented touchpoints into seamless care for better patient outcomes.



The Mission

At Infinitus, our mission is simple: create time for healthcare by making access, adherence, and affordability easier than ever. We’ve automated over 7 million calls and 100M+ minutes of conversation — saving millions of hours for patients and providers.

Backed by over $100M from top investors including Google Ventures, Kleiner Perkins, Andreessen Horowitz, and Coatue, we’re a team that challenges the status quo and moves fast at the intersection of AI and healthcare. Infinitus was recently named to Fast Company's World's Most Innovative Companies and Business Insider’s top startups in healthcare.



Your Role

As a member of the Applied AI team at Infinitus, you will be a Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) who embeds directly with our most strategic customers to drive transformational AI adoption. You will collaborate closely with customer teams to ship advanced AI applications that solve real world business problems. Our FDEs engage with customers to accelerate the adoption of existing products and create new applications built on our platform.



Working closely with our Product, and Engineering teams, you'll combine engineering expertise, an understanding of frontier AI applications, and customer-facing skills to understand customer workflows and develop innovative solutions that address complex business challenges while maintaining our high standards for safety and reliability.



This is an opportunity to be one of our founding FDEs who helps to shape our forward-deployed motion. We expect our FDEs to operate autonomously, thrive under ambiguity, and represent Infinitus at the highest level in customer environments. This is a significant responsibility: you’ll play a key role in championing our mission in the enterprise.



Your Impact

 - Work within customer systems to build production applications with our Studio and Lens platform ensuring that these products meet customer requirements.

 - Deliver technical artifacts for customers like MCP servers, sub-agents, and workflows etc that will be used in production workflows.

 - Provide white glove deployment support for Infinitus products in enterprise environments.

 - Identify and codify repeatable deployment patterns and contribute insights back to our
   Product and Engineering teams.

 - Maintain strong knowledge of the latest developments in LLM capabilities, implementation patterns, and AI product development stacks.

 - Define agentic best practices and guide customers through AI adoption.

 - Build long term relationships with customers and proactively identify new opportunities for AI deployment throughout the lifecycle of an engagement.

 - Be a champion for Infinitus’s mission in the field.


Your Skillset

 - 8+ years of experience in a technical, customer facing role such as Forward Deployed Engineer, or as a Software Engineer with consulting experience. Former technical founders are also encouraged to apply.

 - Production experience with LLMs including advanced prompt engineering, agent development, evaluation frameworks, and deployment at scale.

 - Strong programming skills with proficiency in Python (and ideally in one or more additional languages like Typescript, Java, etc) and experience shipping production applications

 - High agency with an ability to

source_jobs.title: Implementation Manager
source_jobs.description:
Help Build the Future of Healthcare AI

Infinitus is AI communications in service of patients.

Healthcare is inherently complex and siloed. We’re pioneering agentic communications to connect the entire system — any stakeholder, any channel, inbound and out. The result is intelligent care that reduces patient anxiety, increases speed to treatment, and enables teams to scale impact without stretching resources.

Our system combines industry-leading safety guardrails with the largest dynamic knowledge graph built on millions of real-world interactions. It’s why 44% of the healthcare Fortune 50 trusts Infinitus to turn fragmented touchpoints into seamless care for better patient outcomes.



The Mission

At Infinitus, our mission is simple: create time for healthcare by making access, adherence, and affordability easier than ever. We’ve automated over 7 million calls and 100M+ minutes of conversation — saving millions of hours for patients and providers.

Backed by over $100M from top investors including Google Ventures, Kleiner Perkins, Andreessen Horowitz, and Coatue, we’re a team that challenges the status quo and moves fast at the intersection of AI and healthcare. Infinitus was recently named to Fast Company's World's Most Innovative Companies and Business Insider’s top startups in healthcare.



As an Implementation Manager on the Customer Enablement team, you will lead the technical delivery of AI agent and copilot solutions for Big Pharma, Specialty Pharmacy, Pharm Hubs, Payor and Provider clients, with a focus on system integrations and pilot implementations. You’ll serve as a trusted partner to our clients, guiding them through the configuration, integration, and adoption of Infinitus solutions while ensuring seamless, scalable outcomes.

This role combines technical execution, project ownership, client engagement, and cross-functional collaboration. You’ll be instrumental in helping clients unlock the full value of our AI platform in clinical, commercial, and operational use cases.

 

What You’ll Do:

 - Lead Client Implementations & Pilots
   
   - Own the end-to-end delivery for new client integrations and solution pilots.
   
   - Translate business use cases into detailed solution plans.
   
   - Lead implementations for integrations with client systems including CRM (e.g., Salesforce), Electronic Medical Records (EMRs), and data platforms via APIs, CSVs, Smart on FHIR, and other standards
   
   - Coordinate with rest of the customer enablement, internal engineering and product teams to ensure timely, scalable integration rollouts.

 - Project Management
   
   - Manage multiple concurrent client implementations with structured delivery methodology and clear communication.
   
   - Track milestones, risks, and deliverables to ensure predictable outcomes.
   
   - Capture learnings and contribute to continuous improvement of the implementation playbook.

 - Client Engagement
   
   - Act as a consultative partner to Big Pharma, Specialty Pharmacy, Pharm Hubs, Payor and Provider clients; provide clear guidance on system capabilities, integration strategies, and best practices.
   
   - Lead technical client conversations and working sessions, representing Infinitus as a domain expert.
   
   - Support customers through design, build, QA, UAT, go-Live and hypercare cycles, proactively troubleshooting issues and ensuring client readiness for launch.




Minimum Requirements

 - 4–5 years of experience in leading software implementation, technical consulting, or system integration for healthcare clients, ideally with SaaS platforms.

 - Demonstrated ability to project manage and deliver client-facing technical projects end to end.

 - Strong agile and project management skills to deliver customer features in two-week sprints

 - Hands-on experience working with Salesforce or other CRM platforms.

 - Exceptional verbal and written communication skills—able to bridge business and technical stakeholders.

 - Analytical t

source_jobs.title: AI Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Help Build the Future of Healthcare AI

Infinitus is AI communications in service of patients.

Healthcare is inherently complex and siloed. We’re pioneering agentic communications to connect the entire system — any stakeholder, any channel, inbound and out. The result is intelligent care that reduces patient anxiety, increases speed to treatment, and enables teams to scale impact without stretching resources.

Our system combines industry-leading safety guardrails with the largest dynamic knowledge graph built on millions of real-world interactions. It’s why 44% of the healthcare Fortune 50 trusts Infinitus to turn fragmented touchpoints into seamless care for better patient outcomes.



The Mission

At Infinitus, our mission is simple: create time for healthcare by making access, adherence, and affordability easier than ever. We’ve automated over 7 million calls and 100M+ minutes of conversation — saving millions of hours for patients and providers.

Backed by over $100M from top investors including Google Ventures, Kleiner Perkins, Andreessen Horowitz, and Coatue, we’re a team that challenges the status quo and moves fast at the intersection of AI and healthcare. Infinitus was recently named to Fast Company's World's Most Innovative Companies and Business Insider’s top startups in healthcare.



Your Role

We are looking for a Fullstack Software Engineer to help us design, build, and scale the core of our digital health platform. You’ll work on expanding the capabilities of Eva, our AI-powered health assistant, and build systems that automate and simplify healthcare administration at scale. This role blends hands-on backend development with opportunities to influence technical direction and mentor teammates.





YOUR IMPACT

Design, build, and scale AI-powered, user-facing products that make Eva’s healthcare interactions more intelligent, intuitive, and reliable.

Lead the end-to-end delivery of complex full-stack product experiences, from frontend architecture and backend services to APIs, data integrations, and production workflows.

Partner closely with Product, Design, Data Science, Infrastructure, and other engineering teams to bring AI capabilities into real user workflows in a practical, scalable, and measurable way.

Translate ambiguous product and AI opportunities into clear technical plans, balancing speed, user experience, reliability, safety, and long-term maintainability.

Build robust systems that connect application logic, data pipelines, model outputs, and human-centered product experiences.

Evaluate and integrate AI technologies, including LLM-enabled workflows, retrieval systems, classification, summarization, automation, and decision-support experiences where appropriate.

Establish strong frontend and backend engineering standards, including component architecture, API design, system reliability, observability, testing, and development best practices.

Make high-quality technical tradeoffs across product impact, engineering complexity, scalability, and long-term platform health.

Drive cross-team and cross-org alignment to ship high-impact work, influencing through technical clarity, judgment, and collaboration rather than authority.

Mentor and grow engineers across the team, raising the bar for technical execution, product thinking, and engineering culture.

Contribute to both short- and long-term technical roadmaps, especially around AI-enabled product development, platform scalability, and full-stack architecture.

Help shape how Eva responsibly applies AI in healthcare workflows, with a focus on usefulness, trust, reliability, and excellent user experience.




YOUR SKILLSET

8+ years of practical full-stack engineering experience

A track record of leading the design and delivery of complex consumer or B2B user-facing products across the full stack.

Deep expertise in modern frontend and backend development, with strong proficiency in technologies such as React, TypeScript, Python, Go, Java, C++,

source_jobs.title: Data Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Help Build the Future of Healthcare AI

Infinitus is AI communications in service of patients.

Healthcare is inherently complex and siloed. We’re pioneering agentic communications to connect the entire system — any stakeholder, any channel, inbound and out. The result is intelligent care that reduces patient anxiety, increases speed to treatment, and enables teams to scale impact without stretching resources.

Our system combines industry-leading safety guardrails with the largest dynamic knowledge graph built on millions of real-world interactions. It’s why 44% of the healthcare Fortune 50 trusts Infinitus to turn fragmented touchpoints into seamless care for better patient outcomes.



The Mission

At Infinitus, our mission is simple: create time for healthcare by making access, adherence, and affordability easier than ever. We’ve automated over 7 million calls and 100M+ minutes of conversation — saving millions of hours for patients and providers.

Backed by over $100M from top investors including Google Ventures, Kleiner Perkins, Andreessen Horowitz, and Coatue, we’re a team that challenges the status quo and moves fast at the intersection of AI and healthcare. Infinitus was recently named to Fast Company's World's Most Innovative Companies and Business Insider’s top startups in healthcare.



Your Role

We’re looking for a Data Analyst to help guide the evolution of our analytical ecosystem, ensuring our data isn’t just accessible, but deeply insightful and actionable across the company.



In this role, you’ll be hands-on with complex data exploration, advanced modeling, and the translation of messy healthcare data into clear strategic narratives. You’ll partner closely with our product pods and business leaders to solve high-stakes problems, while also helping define the standards for how we model metrics and validate insights. You’ll bring modern practices to how we conduct deep-dive analyses and build scalable reporting, while helping other analysts and stakeholders level up their ability to work with data.



There are also opportunities to develop skills in AI-assisted analytics, analytics engineering (dbt), advanced experimentation design, and in-depth healthcare data governance, should these areas interest you.

Our team’s mission is to deliver high-fidelity insights and intuitive data assets that empower our internal teams to make faster, more confident decisions when identifying product opportunities, troubleshooting operational bottlenecks, and measuring our impact



Your Impact

Build Analytical Capacity: Create the frameworks, metric standards, and shared methodologies that empower partner teams to self-serve and unlock new, scalable use cases for insights.



Shape our Analytical Rigor: Help define our standards for hypothesis testing, experimentation design, and statistical validation. You’ll own the best practices for how we interpret data and ensure "truth" remains consistent as we grow.



Drive Strategic Decisions: Partner with leaders to turn complex data into a clear path forward, providing the insights that help us decide what to build next and where to invest our resources.



Uplift the Craft: Help partner teams design better experiments, interpret data more accurately, and ship better insights through technical guidance, documentation, and training.



Scale the Insight Loop: Expand and maintain automated reporting, alerting, and validation processes that ensure we can monitor performance and identify trends with total confidence.



Ensure Governance by Design: Integrate rigorous data integrity, privacy, and compliance standards into the analytical workflow, ensuring our insights are as secure as they are actionable.




Your Skillset

5+ years of professional experience in data analytics, with a track record of tackling complex problems and delivering high-stakes insights.

Deep fluency in SQL, with the ability to write sophisticated, maintainable queries that serve as a foundation for reliabl

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
Help Build the Future of Healthcare AI

Infinitus is AI communications in service of patients.

Healthcare is inherently complex and siloed. We’re pioneering agentic communications to connect the entire system — any stakeholder, any channel, inbound and out. The result is intelligent care that reduces patient anxiety, increases speed to treatment, and enables teams to scale impact without stretching resources.

Our system combines industry-leading safety guardrails with the largest dynamic knowledge graph built on millions of real-world interactions. It’s why 44% of the healthcare Fortune 50 trusts Infinitus to turn fragmented touchpoints into seamless care for better patient outcomes.



The Mission

At Infinitus, our mission is simple: create time for healthcare by making access, adherence, and affordability easier than ever. We’ve automated over 7 million calls and 100M+ minutes of conversation — saving millions of hours for patients and providers.

Backed by over $100M from top investors including Google Ventures, Kleiner Perkins, Andreessen Horowitz, and Coatue, we’re a team that challenges the status quo and moves fast at the intersection of AI and healthcare. Infinitus was recently named to Fast Company's World's Most Innovative Companies and Business Insider’s top startups in healthcare.



In this role you will own Studio, healthcare’s no-code AI agent builder, and shape how AI agents get built across our platform. You will own the full experience for building AI agents, Workflows and Connectors (MCP Servers) end to end, and you will define how teams design, test, and ship agents that handle real conversations with patients, payors, and providers.

As the Product Owner for Studio, you will own three customer-facing experiences: the AI agent builder, where teams define what an agent does and pressure-tests the full configuration across thousands of simulated conversations; the workflow builder, which connects agents, automated steps, and integrations into coordinated, outcome-driven journeys across the patient, member, payor and provider experience; and connectors, which give agents the same real-time context (eligibility, case status, payor requirements, and more) that our best coordinators rely on. You will own the roadmap, strategy, and success metrics for all three, working side by side with an engineering team applying cutting-edge AI and ML techniques.



Your Impact

 - Own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for Studio, spanning the AI agent builder, workflow builder, and connectors.

 - Define how teams design, test, deploy, and refine AI agents without writing code, and make that experience faster and more powerful with every release.

 - Talk to customers and users constantly, turning what you learn into a roadmap that reflects real needs and unmet opportunities.

 - Partner closely with a deeply technical engineering team, staying current on AI and ML techniques (including reading the research) so you can make sharp product and technical tradeoffs.

 - Own product analytics and customer usage, using data to decide what to build, what to cut, and where to double down.

 - Ship, measure, and iterate quickly, balancing the quality, latency, and cost that healthcare demands.

 - Act as a spokesperson for Studio, internally and with customers, telling a clear story about where the product is headed.



Your Skillset

 - 4 to 6 years of product management experience, with a track record of launching customer-facing products (not just internal tools).

 - Genuine curiosity about AI: you actively learn on your own, through side projects, self-study, or staying close to the latest models and techniques.

 - A data-driven approach to decisions, with strong product analytics instincts.

 - A demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional teams through the full product development lifecycle.

 - Comfort working alongside a highly technical engineering team and keeping pace with a fast-moving AI space.

 - A hustle

source_jobs.title: AI Patient Access Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Help Build the Future of Healthcare AI

Infinitus is AI communications in service of patients.

Healthcare is inherently complex and siloed. We’re pioneering agentic communications to connect the entire system — any stakeholder, any channel, inbound and out. The result is intelligent care that reduces patient anxiety, increases speed to treatment, and enables teams to scale impact without stretching resources.

Our system combines industry-leading safety guardrails with the largest dynamic knowledge graph built on millions of real-world interactions. It’s why 44% of the healthcare Fortune 50 trusts Infinitus to turn fragmented touchpoints into seamless care for better patient outcomes.



The Mission

At Infinitus, our mission is simple: create time for healthcare by making access, adherence, and affordability easier than ever. We’ve automated over 7 million calls and 100M+ minutes of conversation — saving millions of hours for patients and providers.

Backed by over $100M from top investors including Google Ventures, Kleiner Perkins, Andreessen Horowitz, and Coatue, we’re a team that challenges the status quo and moves fast at the intersection of AI and healthcare. Infinitus was recently named to Fast Company's World's Most Innovative Companies and Business Insider’s top startups in healthcare.




ROLE OVERVIEW

This is the next generation of a contact center role in the healthcare field. As an AI Patient Access Specialist, you will serve as the "human-in-the-loop," bridging the gap between traditional insurance verification and cutting-edge machine learning. You won’t just be making calls; you will be teaching the Infinitus AI system how to navigate the complexities of the healthcare reimbursement landscape.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - AI System Navigation: Assist the Infinitus AI system in completing complex insurance verification phone calls through our proprietary platform.

 - Platform Training: Work closely with operations and engineering teams to provide feedback that improves healthcare data collection and AI accuracy.

 - Quality Assurance: Ensure the quality of recorded AI outputs, identifying nuances in payer responses, pushing back as necessary to ensure accurate responses to questions.

 - Data Entry: Ensure accurate entry of information into proprietary platforms from a number of sources including but not limited to faxes, forms, or information shared verbally from callers.

 - Adverse Events Handling: Identify, document, and submit Adverse Events during interaction with callers and/or received documentation.

 - Drive AI Efficiency: Consistently hit daily production and quality benchmarks by swiftly and accurately handling tasks that the AI agent cannot handle.

 - Peak Season Support: Participate in mandatory periodic overtime during high-volume seasons, new product launches, or system extensions to ensure zero backlog.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Domain Expertise: Background in Insurance Verification strongly preferred and a minimum of 2+ years of experience in a healthcare field, contact center, or insurance/patient access contact center preferred.

 - Technical Literacy: Comfortable learning and operating new, proprietary software programs with high speed and precision.

 - Communication: Strong written and verbal communication skills; ability to explain complex insurance concepts clearly.

 - Compliance: Ability to work in strict compliance with HIPAA regulations and safeguard sensitive personal health information (PHI).

 - Detail Orientation: Exceptional attention to detail and the ability to maintain accuracy while managing a high volume of tasks.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in a high-volume Call Center environment.

 - Previous experience as a Case Manager or Reimbursement Specialist.

 - 1–2 years of Customer Service or Sales experience.




CORE COMPETENCIES

 - Problem Solving: The ability to navigate ambiguous payer responses and find solutions.

 - Collaboration: Working effe

source_jobs.title: Operations Manager, Digital Hub
source_jobs.description:
Help Build the Future of Healthcare AI

Infinitus is AI communications in service of patients.

Healthcare is inherently complex and siloed. We’re pioneering agentic communications to connect the entire system — any stakeholder, any channel, inbound and out. The result is intelligent care that reduces patient anxiety, increases speed to treatment, and enables teams to scale impact without stretching resources.

Our system combines industry-leading safety guardrails with the largest dynamic knowledge graph built on millions of real-world interactions. It’s why 44% of the healthcare Fortune 50 trusts Infinitus to turn fragmented touchpoints into seamless care for better patient outcomes.



The Mission

At Infinitus, our mission is simple: create time for healthcare by making access, adherence, and affordability easier than ever. We’ve automated over 7 million calls and 100M+ minutes of conversation — saving millions of hours for patients and providers.

Backed by over $100M from top investors including Google Ventures, Kleiner Perkins, Andreessen Horowitz, and Coatue, we’re a team that challenges the status quo and moves fast at the intersection of AI and healthcare. Infinitus was recently named to Fast Company's World's Most Innovative Companies and Business Insider’s top startups in healthcare.



We are seeking a highly motivated, hands-on Operations Manager to lead the launch and daily activities of our digital patient access hub. This is not a purely strategic role; we need a tactical leader who is ready to roll up their sleeves, get into the weeds, and actively drive the stabilization of a newly launched program.

In this vital role, you will direct the end-to-end operations of a technology-enabled, dedicated patient support program, managing a dynamic, hybrid workforce composed of human-in-the-loop specialists and AI agents. You will ensure the seamless delivery of services including benefits verification, prior authorization support, and care coordination—while optimizing how AI handles routine inquiries, triage, and task automation. The ideal candidate brings a strong background in healthcare contact center operations, experience with AI integration, and the frontline leadership skills necessary to troubleshoot early-stage friction, drive rapid stabilization, and foster human-AI collaboration while meeting strict Service Level Agreements (SLAs).



Key Responsibilities:

Launch Stabilization & Daily Operations

 - Tactical Execution & Stabilization: Actively monitor the "virtual floor," dive into the granular details of complex escalations, and personally troubleshoot workflow bottlenecks to ensure a rapid and smooth transition from launch to steady-state operations.

 - Operational Oversight: Direct the day-to-day operations of the Hub, managing efficient workflow distribution across AI systems and teams while maintaining a constant, hands-on presence to quickly address immediate launch challenges.

 - Seamless Handoffs: Personally investigate friction points in routing protocols between AI agents and human staff, rapidly iterating to minimize disruptions during escalations and complex case transfers.

 - Resource Allocation: Monitor real-time volume across both digital (AI) and traditional channels, jumping into the queues to assess reality on the ground and adjusting human staffing assignments to handle escalations during peak periods.



Team Leadership & Development

 - In-the-Weeds People Management: Direct, coach, and mentor a team of frontline supervisors, team leads, and human-in-the-loop specialists by leading from the front, participating in side-by-side shadowing, and directly handling edge cases when necessary.

 - Human-AI Collaboration: Foster a culture where human staff effectively leverage AI tools. Train employees on how to interact with, monitor, and provide corrective feedback to AI systems based on your own hands-on experience with the tools.

 - Continuous Tuning: Partner with technical and operat

source_jobs.title: Financial Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Help Build the Future of Healthcare AI

Infinitus is AI communications in service of patients.

Healthcare is inherently complex and siloed. We’re pioneering agentic communications to connect the entire system — any stakeholder, any channel, inbound and out. The result is intelligent care that reduces patient anxiety, increases speed to treatment, and enables teams to scale impact without stretching resources.

Our system combines industry-leading safety guardrails with the largest dynamic knowledge graph built on millions of real-world interactions. It’s why 44% of the healthcare Fortune 50 trusts Infinitus to turn fragmented touchpoints into seamless care for better patient outcomes.



The Mission

At Infinitus, our mission is simple: create time for healthcare by making access, adherence, and affordability easier than ever. We’ve automated over 7 million calls and 100M+ minutes of conversation — saving millions of hours for patients and providers.

Backed by over $100M from top investors including Google Ventures, Kleiner Perkins, Andreessen Horowitz, and Coatue, we’re a team that challenges the status quo and moves fast at the intersection of AI and healthcare. Infinitus was recently named to Fast Company's World's Most Innovative Companies and Business Insider’s top startups in healthcare.



We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Financial Analyst to join our finance team. In this role, you will be responsible for managing end-to-end billing processes, vendor invoice processing, and financial reporting activities. You will work closely with internal teams and external partners to ensure smooth financial operations and accurate reporting. The ideal candidate is organized, analytical, and has strong communication skills and attention to detail.

Key Responsibilities:

 1. Customer Billing & Invoicing
    
    - Manage the preparation and issuance of accurate customer invoices.
    
    - Ensure timely collection and reconciliation of accounts.
    
    - Resolve any billing disputes or discrepancies in collaboration with the customer support team.

 2. Vendor Invoice Processing
    
    - Handle the processing of vendor invoices, ensuring compliance with contracts and company policies.
    
    - Maintain records of all vendor transactions, ensuring accurate tracking and payment.

 3. Aging Analysis & Reporting
    
    - Conduct regular accounts receivable aging analysis to identify overdue accounts and optimize cash flow.
    
    - Generate and present financial reports related to billing, procurement, and vendor expenses to senior management.

 4. Business Partnership
    
    - Collaborate with internal departments such as Operations, Sales, and Marketing to provide insights and support decision-making.

 5. Process Improvement
    
    - Identify opportunities to streamline billing, procurement, and financial reporting processes for efficiency.
    
    - Implement best practices to improve the accuracy and timeliness of all transactions.

 6. Variance Analysis
    
    - Conduct thorough variance analysis between actuals and budget/forecasts to identify key drivers.
    
    - Provide actionable insights and explanations for discrepancies and work with business units to address deviations.
    
    - Present findings to senior management in a clear and concise manner.
    
    - Analyze monthly financial results, comparing actual performance against budgets and forecasts.
    
    - Assist in preparing financial reports, including profit and loss statements, balance sheets, and cash flow statements.

 7. Ad-hoc Reporting & Analysis
    
    - Develop ad-hoc financial reports and analyses based on business needs and management requests.
    
    - Provide support for special projects, including financial modeling and scenario analysis.
    
    - Assist in preparing materials for board meetings, investor updates, and other key stakeholders.

 8. Financial Modeling
    
    - Build and maintain robust financi

source_jobs.title: National Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
Help Build the Future of Healthcare AI

Infinitus is AI communications in service of patients.

Healthcare is inherently complex and siloed. We’re pioneering agentic communications to connect the entire system — any stakeholder, any channel, inbound and out. The result is intelligent care that reduces patient anxiety, increases speed to treatment, and enables teams to scale impact without stretching resources.

Our system combines industry-leading safety guardrails with the largest dynamic knowledge graph built on millions of real-world interactions. It’s why 44% of the healthcare Fortune 50 trusts Infinitus to turn fragmented touchpoints into seamless care for better patient outcomes.



The Mission

At Infinitus, our mission is simple: create time for healthcare by making access, adherence, and affordability easier than ever. We’ve automated over 7 million calls and 100M+ minutes of conversation — saving millions of hours for patients and providers.

Backed by over $100M from top investors including Google Ventures, Kleiner Perkins, Andreessen Horowitz, and Coatue, we’re a team that challenges the status quo and moves fast at the intersection of AI and healthcare. Infinitus was recently named to Fast Company's World's Most Innovative Companies and Business Insider’s top startups in healthcare.



We're in search of a knowledgeable sales leader with experience selling multi-year software agreements. Our customers are primarily large pharmaceutical manufacturers, pharmaceutical hubs and specialty pharmacies. Join us to improve back-office operations by automating repetitive phone calls. Our solution frees up our customers’ time so they can spend more time helping patients.



The National Account Executive will be responsible for prospecting and selling Infinitus automation solutions. Additional responsibilities include client and market analysis, analyzing business processes, leading prospecting, and retention. The Account Executive will be a natural relationship builder with a demonstrated ability to nurture executive relationships.



This position is remote from home office, this position may include up to 60% domestic travel.



In this role you will:

 - Achieves sales objectives and quota by providing independent sales coverage of assigned accounts or within assigned territory, as well as client retention of assigned territory.

 - Successfully position value / benefits with “C level” decision makers.

 - Contribute to sales pipeline growth through Growth & Retention initiatives (3-4x quota).Match your deep knowledge of clients to a short- and long-term solution roadmap with expected value and results creating a trusted high-performance partner/advisor relationship.

 - Source new sales opportunities through inbound lead follow-up and outbound cold calls and emails.

We are looking for someone who:

 - Has a demonstrated effective use of selling skills and has consistently achieved or exceeded forecast over a sustained period of time.

 - Has a minimum of 3-5 years selling software and services to the any of: Pharmaceutical manufacturers or Payers.

 - Has successful SP / Health System with a strong track record of consistently meeting and exceeding sales goals.

 - Has a proven ability to position value / benefits with “C level” decision makers; ability to initiate and develop successful selling strategies; ability to fully negotiate contracts and close profitable business.

 - Has a sense of urgency, is action-oriented and results driven.A person with a strong “hands on'' and disciplined work ethic.

 - Someone who sets a quick pace and constantly raises the bar for themselves towards higher levels of achievement.

 - Demonstrates initiative, innovation, continual learning, and personal development.

 - Works well in both individual and team environments.

 - Has excellent verbal and written communication skills.

 - Prefer CST or EST time zones.

Why You’ll Love It Here

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
Help Build the Future of Healthcare AI

Infinitus is AI communications in service of patients.

Healthcare is inherently complex and siloed. We’re pioneering agentic communications to connect the entire system — any stakeholder, any channel, inbound and out. The result is intelligent care that reduces patient anxiety, increases speed to treatment, and enables teams to scale impact without stretching resources.

Our system combines industry-leading safety guardrails with the largest dynamic knowledge graph built on millions of real-world interactions. It’s why 44% of the healthcare Fortune 50 trusts Infinitus to turn fragmented touchpoints into seamless care for better patient outcomes.



The Mission

At Infinitus, our mission is simple: create time for healthcare by making access, adherence, and affordability easier than ever. We’ve automated over 7 million calls and 100M+ minutes of conversation — saving millions of hours for patients and providers.

Backed by over $100M from top investors including Google Ventures, Kleiner Perkins, Andreessen Horowitz, and Coatue, we’re a team that challenges the status quo and moves fast at the intersection of AI and healthcare. Infinitus was recently named to Fast Company's World's Most Innovative Companies and Business Insider’s top startups in healthcare.



At Infinitus, you will:

 - Own enterprise accounts end-to-end: from onboarding through renewal and expansion, serving as the primary strategic contact across the full customer lifecycle

 - Drive ongoing customer health and success: conduct regular check-ins, usage and outcomes monitoring, Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs), and proactive outreach to ensure value realization and early issue detection

 - Identify and convert growth/expansion opportunities: map customer needs, identify new/expansion use cases, and partner with Sales to grow adoption across customers’ business units and departments

 - Support implementation and adoption: partner with our Enablement team on integrations, workflow design, technical launches, and user training to ensure rapid time-to-value and stable onboarding
